repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Hello!

Closes #34102

This adds `nvim://` URI scheme to support "Open in Editor" functionality from external tools (browsers, file managers, error reporters, etc.).

### Format

```
nvim://{cmd}?file={path}[&line={n}][&column={n}][&server={addr}]
```

Where `{cmd}` is one of:
- `edit` - open in current window
- `tabedit` - open in new tab
- `tabnew` - open in new tab (alias)
- `split` - open in horizontal split
- `vsplit` - open in vertical split
- `drop` - reuse existing window or open new
- `open` - use command from `g:uri_opencmd` (default: `edit`)

```lua
vim.g.uri_opencmd = 'tabedit'  -- nvim://open?... will use tabedit
```

This is useful for external tools that want to provide a generic "open in nvim" link without hardcoding a specific command.

### Behavior

1. If `&server={addr}` is provided, connects to that specific instance
2. Otherwise, auto-discovers existing Nvim instances via socket files in `$XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`
3. If a server is found, opens the file there and exits
4. If no server is found, opens the file locally

### Setup

This reuses the existing `nvim.desktop` file. Users can register Nvim as the handler for `nvim://` URIs:

```bash
xdg-mime default nvim.desktop x-scheme-handler/nvim
```

### Note

I had to update the desktop file to `%U` to support this, so I went ahead and added `file://` support as well.

Thanks!

comment_data.body: Should also mention `file://` support here, along with in `starting.txt`?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -385,7 +385,10 @@ PLUGINS
 
 STARTUP
 
-• todo
+• |nvim://| URI scheme for "Open in Editor" integration.  External tools
+  (browsers, file managers) can open files in a running Nvim instance:
+  `nvim://tabedit?file=/path/to/file.txt&line=10`
+  Use `nvim://open?...` with |g:uri_opencmd| for a configurable command.

comment_data.body: ~~add a vim.uri module, the old vim.uri_xx stuff needs to go away (but not in this pr). for this pr, just introduce  vim.uri.parse_nvim~~
@justinmk, the current uri stuff lives in `uri.lua`---should I keep my changes in this file or create a new file?
`{cmd}` should be limited to a fixed list for now, to avoid security holes. https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/38985

comment_data.body: this is a separate topic, isn't it? would reduce friction on this PR to put this in a different PR, because we need to discuss `gf`, `expand('<cfile>')`, etc., rather than adding special-case handling to `main.c` ?

comment_data.body:
this doesn't seem right. see https://github.com/neovim/neovim/pull/38006/changes#r3118294367 and : 

- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/24032
- https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/1281

the handling for `file://` should be in one place, not repeated ad-hoc in many places.  probably in a separate PR.
why is this in editor.lua? should live in `_core/uri.lua`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1214,6 +1214,75 @@ function vim._cs_remote(rcid, server_addr, connect_error, args)
   }
 end
 
+--- Handle nvim:// URI scheme for "Open in Editor" functionality.
+--- Parses the URI and opens the file in an existing Neovim server if available.
+--- If no server is found, opens the file locally.
+---
+--- @param uri string The nvim:// URI to handle
+--- @return boolean|string true=exit after sending to server, false=continue startup, string=error
+function vim._handle_uri(uri)

comment_data.body:
don't need to repeat `x_` for names that live in a `x` module

```suggestion
  local parsed, err = require('vim.uri').parse_nvim(uri)
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1214,6 +1214,75 @@ function vim._cs_remote(rcid, server_addr, connect_error, args)
   }
 end
 
+--- Handle nvim:// URI scheme for "Open in Editor" functionality.
+--- Parses the URI and opens the file in an existing Neovim server if available.
+--- If no server is found, opens the file locally.
+---
+--- @param uri string The nvim:// URI to handle
+--- @return boolean|string true=exit after sending to server, false=continue startup, string=error
+function vim._handle_uri(uri)
+  local parsed, err = require('vim.uri').uri_parse_nvim(uri)

comment_data.body:
can `vim.ui.open()` handle this? we should not need another hook point. users/plugins can override `vim.ui.open` if they want. LMK if I'm missing something.

if `vim.ui.open()` isn't the right "layer", then maybe it's time to introduce `vim.ui.edit()` (name/module TBD). It will handle `nvim://`, and later, will fill the role of `gf`, and even `:edit` could delegate to it.

The main point is that hooks (aka "providers") are exposed as module functions, not global variables.

comment_data.body: put all the error cases in one `it('validation')` case
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -260,4 +260,126 @@ describe('URI methods', function()
       eq('[%3a]', exec_lua('return vim.uri_encode(str, rfc)'))
     end)
   end)
+
+  describe('uri_parse_nvim', function()
+    it('parses basic nvim:// URI with edit command', function()
+      local result = exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://edit?file=/path/to/file.txt')]])
+      eq('edit', result.cmd)
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+      eq(nil, result.line)
+      eq(nil, result.column)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with tabedit command', function()
+      local result =
+        exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://tabedit?file=/path/to/file.txt&line=42')]])
+      eq('tabedit', result.cmd)
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+      eq(42, result.line)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with split command', function()
+      local result = exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://split?file=/path/to/file.txt')]])
+      eq('split', result.cmd)
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with vsplit command', function()
+      local result = exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://vsplit?file=/path/to/file.txt')]])
+      eq('vsplit', result.cmd)
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with line and column', function()
+      local result = exec_lua(
+        [[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://edit?file=/path/to/file.txt&line=42&column=10')]]
+      )
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+      eq(42, result.line)
+      eq(10, result.column)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with server parameter', function()
+      local result = exec_lua(
+        [[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://tabedit?file=/path/to/file.txt&server=/tmp/nvim.sock')]]
+      )
+      eq('/path/to/file.txt', result.file)
+      eq('/tmp/nvim.sock', result.server)
+    end)
+
+    it('parses nvim:// URI with relative path', function()
+      local result =
+        exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://edit?file=./relative/path.txt&line=10')]])
+      eq('./relative/path.txt', result.file)
+      eq(10, result.line)
+    end)
+
+    it('decodes percent-encoded file paths', function()
+      local result =
+        exec_lua([[return vim.uri_parse_nvim('nvim://edit?file=/path/to/my%20file.txt')]])
+      eq('/path/to/my file.txt', result.file)
+    end)
+
+    it('returns error for non-nvim scheme', function()
+      local err = exec_lua([[
+        local _, e = vim.uri_parse_nvim('http://example.com')
+        return e
+      ]])
+      eq('URI scheme must be "nvim"', err)
+    end)
+
+    it('returns error for missing file parameter', function()

comment_data.body: we don't need a separate test description for granular things. just one `it('parses nvim:// URI')` that asserts all the various cases is more readable.

comment_data.body: lots of duplication in these tests, makes it harder to see the important parts
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -204,6 +204,203 @@ describe('command-line option', function()
   end
 end)
 
+describe('file:// URI arguments', function()
+  it('handles file:// URI (file:///path)', function()
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'file uri test')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)
+    end)
+    local uri = 'file://' .. fname
+    clear({ args = { uri } })
+    eq(fname, fn.fnamemodify(fn.expand('%:p'), ':gs?\\?/?'))
+  end)
+
+  it('handles file:// URI with extra slashes', function()
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'file uri test')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)
+    end)
+    local uri = 'file:///' .. fname
+    clear({ args = { uri } })
+    eq(fname, fn.fnamemodify(fn.expand('%:p'), ':gs?\\?/?'))
+  end)
+
+  it('handles multiple file:// URIs', function()
+    local fname1 = t.tmpname()
+    local fname2 = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname1, 'file 1')
+    write_file(fname2, 'file 2')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname1)
+      os.remove(fname2)
+    end)
+    clear({ args = { 'file://' .. fname1, 'file://' .. fname2 } })
+    eq(2, fn.argc())
+    eq(fname1, fn.fnamemodify(fn.argv(0), ':p:gs?\\?/?'))
+    eq(fname2, fn.fnamemodify(fn.argv(1), ':p:gs?\\?/?'))
+  end)
+end)
+
+describe('nvim:// URI scheme', function()
+  local set_session = n.set_session
+  local exec_lua = n.exec_lua
+  local nvim_prog = n.nvim_prog
+
+  local function run_uri(server, uri)
+    set_session(server)
+    local addr = fn.serverlist()[1]
+
+    local client_starter = n.new_session(true)
+    set_session(client_starter)
+    eq(
+      { 0 },
+      exec_lua(
+        [[return vim.fn.jobwait({ vim.fn.jobstart({...}, {
+          stdout_buffered = true,
+          stderr_buffered = true,
+          on_stdout = function(_, data, _)
+            _G.stdout = table.concat(data, '\n')
+          end,
+          on_stderr = function(_, data, _)
+            _G.stderr = table.concat(data, '\n')
+          end,
+        }) })]],
+        nvim_prog,
+        '--clean',
+        '--headless',
+        uri .. '&server=' .. addr
+      )
+    )
+    local res = exec_lua([[return { _G.stdout, _G.stderr }]])
+    client_starter:close()
+    set_session(server)
+    return res
+  end
+
+  it('opens file in existing server with edit', function()
+    local server = n.clear()
+    finally(function()
+      server:close()
+    end)
+
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'nvim uri test content')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)
+    end)
+
+    local uri = 'nvim://edit?file=' .. fname
+    run_uri(server, uri)
+
+    eq(fname, fn.expand('%:p'))
+    eq('nvim uri test content', fn.getline(1))
+  end)
+
+  it('opens file in new tab with tabedit', function()
+    local server = n.clear()
+    finally(function()
+      server:close()
+    end)
+
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'tabedit test content')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)
+    end)
+
+    local uri = 'nvim://tabedit?file=' .. fname
+    run_uri(server, uri)
+
+    eq(2, fn.tabpagenr('$'))
+    eq(fname, fn.expand('%:p'))
+  end)
+
+  it('opens file with line number', function()
+    local server = n.clear()
+    finally(function()
+      server:close()
+    end)
+
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'line1\nline2\nline3\nline4\nline5')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)
+    end)
+
+    local uri = 'nvim://edit?file=' .. fname .. '&line=3'
+    run_uri(server, uri)
+
+    eq(fname, fn.expand('%:p'))
+    eq(3, fn.line('.'))
+  end)
+
+  it('opens file with line and column', function()
+    local server = n.clear()
+    finally(function()
+      server:close()
+    end)
+
+    local fname = t.tmpname()
+    write_file(fname, 'line1\nline2\nline3 with more text\nline4')
+    finally(function()
+      os.remove(fname)

comment_data.body:
what happens if multiple `nvim://` uris are given? is that covered in tests?

note that we have `v:argf` now, I wonder if URIs should be included in it (probably yes). and in that case, maybe this `uri` isn't needed? instead, code that wants to look for the `nvim://` uri(s) can iterate `v:argf` ?
this shouldn't be needed, forward declarations are autogenerated
can't the lua logic do these steps? though "exiting" from lua might be handled a bit later, but still should work i think
oh sorry, we have `vim.uri` already but are doing something weird with it. ignore this for now.

comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -171,7 +171,12 @@ PLUGINS
 
 STARTUP
 
-• todo
+• `file://` URIs passed as command-line arguments are converted to file paths.
+  This enables file managers and `xdg-open` integration.

comment_regular_data.body:
small suggestion, instead of outright rejecting the "open" cmd why not have it fallback to "edit" but allow the handling to be configurable, the option could be named something like `vim.uri.opencmd` and the changes could be something like

```diff
+vim.uri.opencmd = 'edit'

local nvim_uri_cmds = {
  drop = true,
  edit = true,
  split = true,
  tabedit = true,
  tabnew = true,
  vsplit = true,
+  open = true,
}
```

```diff
+    if cmd == 'open' then
+      cmd = vim.uri.opencmd
+    end
+
    return {
      cmd = cmd,
      file = params.file,
      line = tonumber(params.line),
      column = tonumber(params.column),
      server = params.server,
    }
  end
```

yes i know the suggested code is very naive and a check should be done to ensure that `vim.uri.opencmd` is a real valid command but at this suggestion level that's neither here nor there.
@justinmk should the lua logic for this be in `_core/`? I would think so but I'm not as knowledgeable about what should be placed there as you.

> I went ahead and added `file://` support as well.

So I didn't notice this before, but between 0.11 and master, support for `file://` was already added (e.g. `nvim file:///etc/motd` opens `/etc/motd`). I tried to `git bisect` this addition but the feature is old enough that I cannot compile Nvim that far back. What do the changes you made do to improve this feature from how it already exists?

@superatomic, nvim can only load `file://` uris via **netrw**, not natively. And if netrw is disabled then it doesn't work, you can try it for yourself:

```lua
-- minmal.lua
vim.g['loaded_netrw'] = 1 -- disable netrw
```
and then running the following command
```bash
nvim --clean -u minimal.lua file:///some/file
```
Got it! Thank you for clarifying. Interesting that this behavior of netrw was added recently.

@justinmk anything that needs to be done before this can be merged? I'd quite like `file://` support that this PR adds (and although I have less of a use for `nvim://`, it still seems like a good feature).

> why not have it fallback to "edit" but allow the handling to be configurable, the option could be named something like `vim.uri.opencmd`

Not in favor of any configuration for this initial phase. Feel free to make a feature request for that.
Yes sorry been busy---I'll try to get to it this week

repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

title: feat(uri): add nvim:// URI scheme for 'Open in Editor' links
